Phase shift solutions Sm94, Sm97, fa00.
The EDDA detector. It consists of scintillator
bars and rings designed to measure the points, at which
the scattered proton and its recoil partner traverse
the hodoscope.  The kinematic relationship between
these angles are used to identify elastic scattering
events. Additional details see in original paper.
Error estimates for analyzing power include
contributions from the maximum deviations between data
subsets  (<  2.1%), and the impact of the background on
of the excitation functions is +/- 1,2 % in the full
energy range 0.45-2.5 GeV.  It is due to the absolute
normalization uncertainty of +/-1 from the reference
data and the relative errors of the data at 730 MeV.
The uncertainty is not included in main table.
